L1.area: Map rendering engine and native library integration

L2.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists where large image dimensions cause an arithmetic overflow during buffer size calculation, resulting in an undersized memory allocation and subsequent out-of-bounds writes that can crash the process.

L3.area: Build configuration and native rendering library version management
L3.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the bundled native map rendering library where large image height and width values used when constructing a new image object cause an arithmetic overflow during buffer size calculation, resulting in an undersized allocation and out-of-bounds writes that can crash the Mapbox rendering process.

ground_truth.reason: An integer overflow in the bundled gl-native library occurs when large image width and height values are used to create a new Image, causing the computed buffer size to wrap around and allocate an undersized buffer, leading to out-of-bounds writes.
